name: Hetzner Cloud API
what_it_does: German cloud provider offering high-performance, affordable VPS (CX series), dedicated servers, Hetzner Cloud Kubernetes (via hcloud-csi/flannel), load balancers, firewalls, volumes, and private networks. REST API at api.hetzner.cloud with official Python and Go SDKs. Known for exceptional price-to-performance ratio.
use_cases: Spinning up compute instances for agent workloads at a fraction of AWS/GCP cost
Managing Kubernetes clusters via Hetzner Cloud integration with k3s or kubeadm
Building self-hosted AI inference infrastructure with affordable GPU-capable servers
Managing server lifecycle (create, resize, snapshot, rebuild, delete) in agent-driven pipelines
Networking automation: firewalls, load balancers, private networks via API
not_for: Workloads requiring AWS-specific managed services (RDS, SageMaker, Lambda)
US compliance requirements (primary data centers are EU-based, with limited US presence)
Globally distributed workloads needing many regions (Hetzner has fewer than hyperscalers)
Organizations requiring FedRAMP, HIPAA, or SOC1 compliance
best_when: You want affordable, high-quality European cloud infrastructure with an excellent REST API for cost-sensitive compute workloads, self-hosted Kubernetes, or AI inference, and don't need the breadth of managed services from hyperscalers.
avoid_when: You need global presence, advanced managed services, or US-specific compliance certifications. Also avoid for production workloads requiring >99.9% SLA with contractual backing.

auth.notes: API tokens are project-scoped with read or read-write permission only — no fine-grained resource-level scopes. Tokens created per project in Hetzner Cloud Console. Simple bearer token auth: Authorization: Bearer <token>. For least privilege, create separate projects for separate agent workloads.

pricing.paid_tiers: CX22 (shared, 2 vCPU, 4GB RAM) from €3.79/month; CCX13 (dedicated, 2 vCPU) from €12.49/month; GPU servers (A100) available; Load balancers from €5.39/month
pricing.notes: Exceptional price-to-performance — typically 3-5x cheaper than AWS/GCP/Azure for equivalent compute. Billing is hourly. No free tier but new account credits sometimes offered via partner programs. IPv4 is now €0.001/hour extra; IPv6 is free.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API returns structured JSON errors with code and message fields. Error codes are well-documented and descriptive (e.g., resource_limit_exceeded, invalid_input, uniqueness_error). HTTP status codes are used correctly. Error responses consistently include an error object with code and message.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Server creation with the same name returns a conflict (uniqueness_error) — not idempotent. Power on/off actions on already-in-state resources return success (effectively idempotent). Snapshot creation is not idempotent. Agents should check resource existence before create operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Most write operations are asynchronous — they return an action ID that must be polled for completion before proceeding
Server names must be unique within a project — always check before creating to avoid uniqueness_error
Rate limit of 3,600/hour is generous but bulk provisioning agents must still track and throttle
Firewall rules are applied at the project network level — confirm correct server attachment after firewall assignment
IPv4 now costs extra per server hour — agents provisioning many servers should use IPv6 where possible
Webhooks not supported — agents must poll action status or use event log endpoints for async completion detection
Server deletion is immediate and irreversible — agents must confirm before deleting

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced everywhere. Project-scoped tokens with read vs read-write only — no resource-level fine-grained scopes. For multi-tenant agent architectures, use separate Hetzner projects per agent to approximate isolation. ISO27001 certified. GDPR compliant with EU-based data centers. No native secrets management.
